content:
# Spoke Design Review Fixes (2026-03-28)

All 8 Critical, 13 Important, 14 Low, and 10 Minor issues from the 5-persona review have been addressed.

## Key Security Fixes
- MCP bearer token auth for write operations (generated at startup, injected into spokes)
- Per-spoke cryptographic agent tokens (agent_id + bcrypt hash, validated on writes)
- Non-root spoke containers (USER spoke in Dockerfile)
- Scoped GITHUB_TOKEN (fine-grained PAT, documented minimum permissions)
- Pinned Claude Code version (never @latest at runtime)
- Restricted Bash permissions (allow-list of git/cargo/npm/gh, deny docker/ssh)

## Key Reliability Fixes
- HubContext returns Result, never panics
- Duplicate spoke name guard with --force flag
- Network selection: filter system networks, prefer devcontainer, require config if ambiguous
- Container detection check (/.dockerenv or /proc/1/cgroup)
- Pre-flight validation (Docker, credentials, GITHUB_TOKEN, disk space)
- Startup health gate (wait for hub MCP before starting Claude Code)
- Trap-based error reporting to corvia on entrypoint failure
- Default resource limits (4GB memory, 512 cpu_shares, log rotation)
- Destroy --all requires confirmation
- Git clone retry with 3 attempts

## Key UX Fixes
- spoke list shows failure reason column
- Branch naming from issue title via gh API
- Default branch queried dynamically (not hardcoded master)
- spoke restart command (preserves repo checkout)
- spoke check command (validates environment)

Doc: docs/decisions/2026-03-28-spoke-design-review-fixes.md
